subject: Physical Education
chapter: Yoga as Preventive Measure for Lifestyle Diseases

cases.passage: Rahul, a 35-year-old IT professional, had been experiencing frequent headaches and fatigue due to prolonged sitting hours at work. His wife, Priya, suggested that he practice yoga to manage stress and prevent lifestyle diseases. With some initial resistance, Rahul started practicing yoga for 30 minutes every morning. He began with simple asanas like Mountain Pose, Downward-Facing Dog, and Cobra Pose to improve his flexibility and balance. Over time, he noticed a significant reduction in his stress levels and an increase in his energy levels. He felt more focused and productive at work, and his relationships with his family and friends improved. As a result, Rahul became a regular yoga practitioner and encouraged his colleagues to adopt a similar lifestyle.

questions.q: Why did Rahul initially resist practicing yoga?
opts: (A) He was not interested in yoga
(B) He was too busy with work
(C) He had no knowledge about yoga
(D) He was not aware of its benefits
questions.explanation: Rahul had initial resistance due to lack of interest in yoga, but later understood its benefits.

questions.q: What were the initial asanas that Rahul practiced?
opts: (A) Mountain Pose, Downward-Facing Dog, and Cobra Pose
(B) Warrior Pose, Triangle Pose, and Tree Pose
(C) Plank Pose, Boat Pose, and Eagle Pose
(D) Headstand, Shoulder Stand, and Bridge Pose
questions.explanation: Rahul started with simple asanas like Mountain Pose, Downward-Facing Dog, and Cobra Pose.

questions.q: How did Rahul's relationships with his family and friends improve?
opts: (A) He became more aggressive and short-tempered
(B) He became more outgoing and social
(C) They became more understanding and supportive
(D) He became more isolated and withdrawn
questions.explanation: Rahul's relationships improved as he became more focused and productive at work, and his stress levels reduced.

questions.q: What was the outcome of Rahul's regular yoga practice?
opts: (A) He became more stressed and anxious
(B) He experienced no change
(C) He noticed a significant reduction in stress levels and an increase in energy levels
(D) He became more lethargic and lazy
questions.explanation: Rahul noticed a significant reduction in stress levels and an increase in energy levels after regular yoga practice.

questions.q: Why did Rahul encourage his colleagues to adopt a similar lifestyle?
opts: (A) He wanted to show off his yoga skills
(B) He wanted to impress his colleagues
(C) He wanted to help them manage stress and prevent lifestyle diseases
(D) He wanted to start a yoga business
questions.explanation: Rahul encouraged his colleagues to adopt a similar lifestyle to help them manage stress and prevent lifestyle diseases.

cases.passage: Sneha, a 28-year-old marketing executive, had been experiencing chronic back pain and sciatica due to her sedentary lifestyle. She tried various treatments, including medication and physical therapy, but found no relief. A friend recommended that she try yoga to improve her flexibility and reduce her back pain. Sneha started practicing yoga with a qualified instructor and incorporated it into her daily routine. She learned various asanas, including Child's Pose, Cat-Cow Pose, and Seated Forward Fold, to stretch her back and improve her posture. Over time, Sneha noticed a significant reduction in her back pain and sciatica, and she felt more energetic and focused. She also found it easier to manage her stress levels and sleep better at night.

opts: (A) She wanted to improve her physical strength
(B) She wanted to reduce her back pain and sciatica
(C) She wanted to increase her flexibility and range of motion
(D) She wanted to improve her mental health
questions.explanation: Sneha tried yoga to reduce her back pain and sciatica due to her sedentary lifestyle.

questions.q: What asanas did Sneha learn to stretch her back?
opts: (A) Child's Pose, Cat-Cow Pose, and Seated Forward Fold
(B) Warrior Pose, Triangle Pose, and Tree Pose
(C) Plank Pose, Boat Pose, and Eagle Pose
(D) Headstand, Shoulder Stand, and Bridge Pose
questions.explanation: Sneha learned Child's Pose, Cat-Cow Pose, and Seated Forward Fold to stretch her back.

questions.q: How did Sneha's back pain and sciatica change after she started practicing yoga?
opts: (A) They worsened
(B) They remained the same
(C) They significantly reduced
(D) They completely disappeared
questions.explanation: Sneha's back pain and sciatica significantly reduced after she started practicing yoga.

questions.q: What were the benefits that Sneha experienced after practicing yoga?
opts: (A) She felt more energetic and focused, and her stress levels reduced
(B) She felt more tired and lethargic, and her stress levels increased
(C) She experienced no change
(D) She became more isolated and withdrawn
questions.explanation: Sneha experienced a reduction in back pain, sciatica, and stress levels, and an increase in energy and focus after practicing yoga.

questions.q: Why was it easier for Sneha to manage her stress levels after practicing yoga?
opts: (A) She was more stressed and anxious
(B) She was more overwhelmed and busy
(C) She was more relaxed and calm
(D) She was more aggressive and short-tempered
questions.explanation: Sneha found it easier to manage her stress levels after practicing yoga due to reduced stress and increased relaxation.
